texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Feasibility research of potable water production via solar-heated hollow fiber membrane distillation system
texts.text: Feasibility research of potable water production via solar-heated hollow fiber membrane distillation system

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Xuyun Wang a,b , Lin Zhang a , Huajian Yang a , Huanlin Chen a*
texts.text: Xuyun Wang a,b , Lin Zhang a , Huajian Yang a , Huanlin Chen a*

texts.label: text
texts.orig: College of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Zhejiang University, Hangzhou, China, 310027 b College of Chemical Engineering, Qingdao University of Science and Technology, Qingdao, China, 266042
texts.text: College of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Zhejiang University, Hangzhou, China, 310027 b College of Chemical Engineering, Qingdao University of Science and Technology, Qingdao, China, 266042

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Nowadays, more than 110 cities of China confront the serious problem of fresh water shortage, and more than 70% of the rivers and lakes are contaminated to some extent. About 38 million people are depending on brackish water for life. Desalination of seawater and brackish water is one of the ways to solve the potable water shortage problem of the people living in arid areas or by the sea. A solar-heated hollow fiber vacuum membrane distillation (VMD) system for potable water production from underground water was designed. The solar radiation time and mean temperature of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005 were analyzed. The performance of the system for potable water production was tested. The experiment results showed that the pure water flux of the system could reach 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (per square meter membrane area with the 8 m 2 solar energy collector) in October 2006 in Hangzhou area, south of China. The daily cumulated permeation flux could be more than 170 kg m � 2 (membrane area) in October 2006 under good weather conditions, and more than 50 kg m � 2 in cloudy weather in Hangzhou area of China. The influence of the feed flow rate on the permeate flux is analyzed. The results show that the higher the feed temperature, the lesser the temperature polarization influence on the permeate flux of the VMD. The construction of the presented system is simple and compact with operation, and maintenance costs mainly for pumps. The feasibility of the hollow fiber vacuum MD module coupled with a solar energy collector to produce potable water from underground water has been proven.
texts.text: Nowadays, more than 110 cities of China confront the serious problem of fresh water shortage, and more than 70% of the rivers and lakes are contaminated to some extent. About 38 million people are depending on brackish water for life. Desalination of seawater and brackish water is one of the ways to solve the potable water shortage problem of the people living in arid areas or by the sea. A solar-heated hollow fiber vacuum membrane distillation (VMD) system for potable water production from underground water was designed. The solar radiation time and mean temperature of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005 were analyzed. The performance of the system for potable water production was tested. The experiment results showed that the pure water flux of the system could reach 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (per square meter membrane area with the 8 m 2 solar energy collector) in October 2006 in Hangzhou area, south of China. The daily cumulated permeation flux could be more than 170 kg m � 2 (membrane area) in October 2006 under good weather conditions, and more than 50 kg m � 2 in cloudy weather in Hangzhou area of China. The influence of the feed flow rate on the permeate flux is analyzed. The results show that the higher the feed temperature, the lesser the temperature polarization influence on the permeate flux of the VMD. The construction of the presented system is simple and compact with operation, and maintenance costs mainly for pumps. The feasibility of the hollow fiber vacuum MD module coupled with a solar energy collector to produce potable water from underground water has been proven.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Shandong and Hebei province, excessive extractions have led to a sharp lowering of the water table and of the local ground surface level. According to government statistics, 400 cities are now confronting a fresh water shortage, among which 110 cities are facing especially serious problems. In addition, about 38 million people are depending on brackish water for life. The pollution of water resource becomes more and more serious recently, and more than 70% of the rivers and lakes were contaminated to some extent. So, the problem of potable water in all over China is becoming more and more severe.

In addition, about 38 million people are depending on brackish water for life. The pollution of water resource becomes more and more serious recently, and more than 70% of the rivers and lakes were contaminated to some extent. So, the problem of potable water in all over China is becoming more and more severe.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As a supporting technique for water purification, various types of solar stills have been developed and are being applied worldwide [2-5]. Generally, solar still systems have the advantage of low operating and maintenance costs and the shortcoming of low thermal efficiencies. On the other hand, membrane technology in desalination is another approach for water purification. Compared with the traditional solar still system, the membrane based desalination processes generally require much smaller installation areas and lower initial investment cost, but the operation maintenance costs are comparatively high. Therefore, a coupling of these two desalination techniques is expected to integrate the advantages of each other solving the problem of potable water shortage.
texts.text: As a supporting technique for water purification, various types of solar stills have been developed and are being applied worldwide [2-5]. Generally, solar still systems have the advantage of low operating and maintenance costs and the shortcoming of low thermal efficiencies. On the other hand, membrane technology in desalination is another approach for water purification. Compared with the traditional solar still system, the membrane based desalination processes generally require much smaller installation areas and lower initial investment cost, but the operation maintenance costs are comparatively high. Therefore, a coupling of these two desalination techniques is expected to integrate the advantages of each other solving the problem of potable water shortage.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In general, there are three approaches coupling solar energy with membrane separation technology for the purpose of water purification: Reverse Osmosis driven by solar photovoltaic panels (ROPV) [6], Photovoltaic electrodialysis system [7] and Membrane Distillation (MD) coupled to solar heat collectors. For small-scale desalination apparatus, the investment cost for solar photovoltaic panels are comparatively high, as well as their operation cost for the feed pretreatment of RO. Therefore, the small-scale application of RO or electrodialysis driven by solar photovoltaic is sparse for the brackish water desalination.
texts.text: In general, there are three approaches coupling solar energy with membrane separation technology for the purpose of water purification: Reverse Osmosis driven by solar photovoltaic panels (ROPV) [6], Photovoltaic electrodialysis system [7] and Membrane Distillation (MD) coupled to solar heat collectors. For small-scale desalination apparatus, the investment cost for solar photovoltaic panels are comparatively high, as well as their operation cost for the feed pretreatment of RO. Therefore, the small-scale application of RO or electrodialysis driven by solar photovoltaic is sparse for the brackish water desalination.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the last decade, feasibility studies of a solar energy-powered MD system for sea water or brackish water desalination for potable water production have been carried out in many countries. Cabassud et al. [8] analyzed the energy consumption for the desalination of simulated seawater using vacuum MD via calculation. F. Banat et al. [9] evaluated the technical feasibility of producing potable water from simulated seawater by integrating a MD module with a solar still. Z.W. Ding et al. [10] developed a mathematical model of solar-powered membrane distillation (SPMD) for the production of pure water from brackish water. Hogan [11] investigated the feasibility of a solar-powered desalination plant for domestic drinking water in arid rural regions of Australia. J. Koschikowski et al. [12] carried out experimental investigations for the desalination of brackish water or seawater to provide potable water using a solar thermal-driven spiral-wound PTFE MD module. The experimental and simulated results in the literature have revealed that the cost of the solar- powered MD system for potable water is comparatively low and the efficiency is greater than with solar stills. However, the output flux of the system is not as high as that of RO.
texts.text: In the last decade, feasibility studies of a solar energy-powered MD system for sea water or brackish water desalination for potable water production have been carried out in many countries. Cabassud et al. [8] analyzed the energy consumption for the desalination of simulated seawater using vacuum MD via calculation. F. Banat et al. [9] evaluated the technical feasibility of producing potable water from simulated seawater by integrating a MD module with a solar still. Z.W. Ding et al. [10] developed a mathematical model of solar-powered membrane distillation (SPMD) for the production of pure water from brackish water. Hogan [11] investigated the feasibility of a solar-powered desalination plant for domestic drinking water in arid rural regions of Australia. J. Koschikowski et al. [12] carried out experimental investigations for the desalination of brackish water or seawater to provide potable water using a solar thermal-driven spiral-wound PTFE MD module. The experimental and simulated results in the literature have revealed that the cost of the solar- powered MD system for potable water is comparatively low and the efficiency is greater than with solar stills. However, the output flux of the system is not as high as that of RO.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The objective of the present work was to investigate the feasibility of coupling a solar energy collector with a hollow fiber vacuum MD module to produce potable water from underground water. The statistics of solar radiation and mean temperature in different months in Hangzhou area in the whole year were analyzed, and the performance of the solar-powered MD system was examined. Theresults proved the feasibility of producing potable water by the couple of a hollow fiber VMD module and a solar energy collector.
texts.text: The objective of the present work was to investigate the feasibility of coupling a solar energy collector with a hollow fiber vacuum MD module to produce potable water from underground water. The statistics of solar radiation and mean temperature in different months in Hangzhou area in the whole year were analyzed, and the performance of the solar-powered MD system was examined. Theresults proved the feasibility of producing potable water by the couple of a hollow fiber VMD module and a solar energy collector.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: related with the local solar radiation time. Figure 1 shows the statistic of solar radiation time of Hangzhou area (in Zhejiang province, south area of China) in 2005. The longest solar radiation time, 242 h, appeared in September. The second longest was 225 h in April. The shortest solar radiation time, only 60 h, appeared in January and February. The total solar radiation time in 2005 was 1993 h, which means that Hangzhou area ranks as the fourth class area of China viewing by solar energy resource distribution (the yearly solar radiation time for first to fifth rank are 3200-3300, 3000-3200, 2200-3000, 14002200 and 1000-1400 h, respectively).
texts.text: related with the local solar radiation time. Figure 1 shows the statistic of solar radiation time of Hangzhou area (in Zhejiang province, south area of China) in 2005. The longest solar radiation time, 242 h, appeared in September. The second longest was 225 h in April. The shortest solar radiation time, only 60 h, appeared in January and February. The total solar radiation time in 2005 was 1993 h, which means that Hangzhou area ranks as the fourth class area of China viewing by solar energy resource distribution (the yearly solar radiation time for first to fifth rank are 3200-3300, 3000-3200, 2200-3000, 14002200 and 1000-1400 h, respectively).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 2 shows the statistic of mean temperature of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005. As shown, though the solar radiation time in July ranked the fourth, the mean temperature was the highest in the whole year, reaching 29.74 8 C. The lowest mean temperature appeared in January, which was only 3.15 8 C. In September, which had the longest solar radiation time, the mean temperature was 27.07 8 C. The output of the solar-heated MD system is related with not only the local solar radiation time and intensity, but also the environment temperature. The solar radiation time and mean temperature in October were close to their respective yearly average.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: MD is a process in which two liquids or solutions at different temperatures are separated by a porous membrane. The temperature difference across the membrane results in a vapor pressure difference and the transport of vapor molecules through membrane pores from the high vapor pressure side to the low vapor pressure side. Compared with other membrane separation technologies, the main advantages of MD are:
texts.text: MD is a process in which two liquids or solutions at different temperatures are separated by a porous membrane. The temperature difference across the membrane results in a vapor pressure difference and the transport of vapor molecules through membrane pores from the high vapor pressure side to the low vapor pressure side. Compared with other membrane separation technologies, the main advantages of MD are:

texts.orig: · The feed need not be strictly pre-treated.
texts.text: The feed need not be strictly pre-treated.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It is less affected by membrane fouling.
texts.text: It is less affected by membrane fouling.

texts.text: It can be operated at constant pressure.

texts.orig: The flow sheet of the solar-heated MD system used in the experiments is shown in Fig. 3.
texts.text: The flow sheet of the solar-heated MD system used in the experiments is shown in Fig. 3.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The system has four major components, a solar energy collector, a hollow fiber membrane module, a permeation condenser and two mechanical pumps. The area of the solar energy collector is 8 m 2 , and the conversion efficiency of the heat collector from light to heat is more than 90%. The hollow fiber membrane module was designed and prepared by the authors themselves. The membrane is micro-porous hollow fiber membrane made from polypropylene, with the inner diameter 371 m m, wall thickness 35 m m, and fiber operative length 0.14 m. The membrane mean pore size is 0.1 m m, and the
texts.text: The system has four major components, a solar energy collector, a hollow fiber membrane module, a permeation condenser and two mechanical pumps. The area of the solar energy collector is 8 m 2 , and the conversion efficiency of the heat collector from light to heat is more than 90%. The hollow fiber membrane module was designed and prepared by the authors themselves. The membrane is micro-porous hollow fiber membrane made from polypropylene, with the inner diameter 371 m m, wall thickness 35 m m, and fiber operative length 0.14 m. The membrane mean pore size is 0.1 m m, and the

texts.orig: Waterquality was determined by conductivity meter. The measured conductivity of feed (underground water) was more than 230 m s cm � 1 , and that of produced water was no more than 4 m s cm � 1 , lower than the Chinese conductivity standard of potable water (10 m s cm � 1 ).
texts.text: Waterquality was determined by conductivity meter. The measured conductivity of feed (underground water) was more than 230 m s cm � 1 , and that of produced water was no more than 4 m s cm � 1 , lower than the Chinese conductivity standard of potable water (10 m s cm � 1 ).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table 1 presents the results of calculated pure water output per day for different months based on the statistic of the mean solar radiation intensity of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005. The calculation was based on the assumption that the radiation time is 9 h for every day for the case of the 8 m 2 area of solar energy collector. Considering the factors of heat loss and actual weather conditions, the final designed output value was obtained by multiplying the calculated value by the factor 0.5. The results show that, in July and August, the designed output of potable water is more than 280 kg d � 1 . In October, the designed output of potable water is 174.6 kg d � 1 , nearly the average of the year.
texts.text: Table 1 presents the results of calculated pure water output per day for different months based on the statistic of the mean solar radiation intensity of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005. The results show that, in July and August, the designed output of potable water is more than 280 kg d � 1 . In October, the designed output of potable water is 174.6 kg d � 1 , nearly the average of the year.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 4 shows the temperature of the water flowing out of the solar energy collector, the feed temperature for the VMD module and the outlet temperature of water for the VMD module on 16 October, 2006. The temperatures were recorded from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM. The weather of the investigated day was sunny. As shown in Fig. 4, the temperature of water flowing out of the collector increased with the increase in solar radiation intensity. It was up to the highest temperature at 12:30 (89 8 C) with the corresponding feed temperature of the hollow fiber membrane module being 83 8 C. The temperature of water flowing out of the collector was kept over 80 8 C and the feed temperature over 70 8 C during 11:30 AM to 4:00 PM. Then, with the solar radiation intensity decreasing, the temperatures began to decrease sharply after 4:00 PM but kept at 60 8 C and 55.5 8 C,

texts.orig: The performance of the solar-heated VMD system for fresh water production, operated at different weather conditions in October 2006, is shown in Figs 5 and 6, where A is for 9 Occtober 2006, cloudy; B for 10 October 2006, cloudy; C for 12 October 2006, fine to cloudy; D and E for 15 October 2006 and 16 October 2006, respectively, fine day; and F 19 October 2006, cloudy. With a feed mass flow rate of about 180 kg h � 1 , the largest permeation flux, 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (curve D of Fig. 5), occurred at 1:00 PM on 16 October
texts.text: The performance of the solar-heated VMD system for fresh water production, operated at different weather conditions in October 2006, is shown in Figs 5 and 6, where A is for 9 Occtober 2006, cloudy; B for 10 October 2006, cloudy; C for 12 October 2006, fine to cloudy; D and E for 15 October 2006 and 16 October 2006, respectively, fine day; and F 19 October 2006, cloudy. With a feed mass flow rate of about 180 kg h � 1 , the largest permeation flux, 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (curve D of Fig. 5), occurred at 1:00 PM on 16 October

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2006 when VMD system operation began, but the fluxes decreased sharply afterward, so the cumulated flux of that day was only up to 118.7 kg m � 2 , as shown in Fig. 6. The largest cumulated flux occurred on 16 October 2006 and was up to 173.5 kg m � 2 (curve E of Fig. 6), with the VMD system operated from 12:00 AM. On that day, the largest flux was 29.75 kg m � 2 h � 1 (curve E of Fig. 5), less than that of the curve D, but the flux decreased more slowly before 3:00 PM. In cloudy days, the largest flux was more than 15 kg m � 2 h � 1 , and the cumulated flux was nearly 50 kg m � 2 . Overall, the experimental results show that the optimum start time for the system operation was 12:00 AM.

Overall, the experimental results show that the optimum start time for the system operation was 12:00 AM.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the VMD operation in the interval (11:00-20:00), the overall energy consumption was 127.54 kWh, with the heat energy being 122.59 kWh; the circulation and vacuum pump energies were 3.33 and 1.62 kWh, respectively.
texts.text: For the VMD operation in the interval (11:00-20:00), the overall energy consumption was 127.54 kWh, with the heat energy being 122.59 kWh; the circulation and vacuum pump energies were 3.33 and 1.62 kWh, respectively.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The experiments were carried out with a small hollow fiber MD module. In order to put the system into real application, the membrane area must be scaled up. Many factors affecting the efficiency of the system during scaling up (loading density of hollow fiber membranes, module configuration, and so on) require specific researches in follow-up studies aimed at achieving an optimal system design.
texts.text: The experiments were carried out with a small hollow fiber MD module. In order to put the system into real application, the membrane area must be scaled up. Many factors affecting the efficiency of the system during scaling up (loading density of hollow fiber membranes, module configuration, and so on) require specific researches in follow-up studies aimed at achieving an optimal system design.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A solar-heated hollow fiber-based VMD system for potable water production from underground water was designed and tested. The system includes four major components, a solar energy collector, a hollow fiber membrane module, permeation condenser and mechanical
texts.text: A solar-heated hollow fiber-based VMD system for potable water production from underground water was designed and tested. The system includes four major components, a solar energy collector, a hollow fiber membrane module, permeation condenser and mechanical

texts.label: text
texts.orig: pumps. The statistics of solar radiation time and mean temperature of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005 were analyzed. The performance of the system was tested in different weather conditions in October 2006. With a feed mass flow rate about 180 kg h � 1 , the largest fresh-water permeate flux is 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (the system is operated from 1:00 PM with an 8 m 2 solar energy collector). Under similar conditions, with the VMD system operated from 12:00 AM, the largest flux is 29.75 kg m � 2 h � 1 , but the daily cumulated flux, 173.5 kg m � 2 , was the largest. The optimum start time for system operation is 12:00 AM. The higher the feed temperature, the lesser the temperature polarization influence on the permeate flux of the VMD.
texts.text: pumps. The statistics of solar radiation time and mean temperature of different months in Hangzhou area in 2005 were analyzed. The performance of the system was tested in different weather conditions in October 2006. With a feed mass flow rate about 180 kg h � 1 , the largest fresh-water permeate flux is 32.19 kg m � 2 h � 1 (the system is operated from 1:00 PM with an 8 m 2 solar energy collector). Under similar conditions, with the VMD system operated from 12:00 AM, the largest flux is 29.75 kg m � 2 h � 1 , but the daily cumulated flux, 173.5 kg m � 2 , was the largest. The optimum start time for system operation is 12:00 AM. The higher the feed temperature, the lesser the temperature polarization influence on the permeate flux of the VMD.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The electric energy efficiency of the present VMD system is satisfying. However, the main goal of the present work was to demonstrate the feasibility of a solar-heated VMD system using an original hollow fiber membrane module constructed by the authors. It is the authors' hope and belief that by scaling up and technological development, the present desalting technology will provide a useful means for solving water shortage problems in the future.
texts.text: The electric energy efficiency of the present VMD system is satisfying. However, the main goal of the present work was to demonstrate the feasibility of a solar-heated VMD system using an original hollow fiber membrane module constructed by the authors. It is the authors' hope and belief that by scaling up and technological development, the present desalting technology will provide a useful means for solving water shortage problems in the future.
